comment.body: 2) I have this backwards I think.  If SPX moves lower, yields move higher which seems counterintuitive.   Yields higher mean bond prices lower so not acting as a good risk off asset.  Bonds yields up could be from increasing term premia (US govt debt risk) or USD lower risk (probably both)

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
1) there is a consumer basket, and bureu meassures its rate of change MoM YoY. 
Core is ex energy yes, less volatile.
Lower than expected = inflation rate of change is slowing. even only on paper... doesn't matter now.

2) spy/yields beta negative... yields down - equities up: future cash flows are discounted at a lower rate so equity PV rises. 
in this case, a cooler inflation rate is being read as lower terminal/forward rates, implying lower yields, impliying higher equities... meaning: market doesn't fear inflation, they want cut and stimulus.
Bond market always knows! rule of thumb.

market wants cooler numbers and weakening growth datas bcs it gives room for the fed to ease.
Bad news (cooling eco) = good news (more room for easing cycle)
